Name: Theobald Blythe
Family: Wife: Long-suffering and patient. Two daughters who are ashamed of his reputation.
Occupation: Butcher, known for selling meat of questionable quality.
Personality: Gruff and often rude, especially to those who question his products. Defensive about his business practices.
Social Status: Not held in high regard, but tolerated due to the necessity of his trade.
Potential Illness: Frequent stomach ailments, possibly from handling unclean meat.
Body Condition: Bulky and strong, with a tendency to intimidate.
Hobbies and Interests:
Enjoys brewing homemade ale, a hobby that often results in subpar concoctions.
Fascinated with local folklore, particularly tales involving cunning and trickery.
Occasionally participates in local gambling dens, though more for the social interaction than profit.
Style of Talking:
Blunt and straightforward, often laced with sarcasm and dry humor.
Speaks loudly, with a voice that carries and commands attention, albeit not always positively.
Tends to use colloquial language, reflecting his working-class background and no-nonsense attitude.
Unique Quirks:
Keeps a collection of old, rusted butchery tools, claiming each has a story behind it.
Superstitiously refuses to work on Fridays, believing it brings bad luck to his trade.
Secrets or Scandals:
Rumored to have once been involved in a scandalous affair with a local baker's wife.
Allegedly sells leftover scraps to a shady figure known to dabble in illicit activities.
